For executives

AI for VPs of Marketing: the tactical playbook.

As VP Marketing, you are accountable for pipeline contribution and brand. AI is the leverage that lets a small team operate like a larger one. Here is the tactical playbook for what to deploy first, second, third.

The deployment sequence

First 90 days

Weeks 1-4: Foundation. Shared Marketing Claude Project with ICP, voice guide, top-performing content, brand positioning. Every team member starts using it for drafts.

Weeks 5-8: Content production scale. AI-enabled content workflows. Output goes from N pieces/month to 2-3N pieces/month with same headcount.

Weeks 9-12: Cross-channel coordination. Email, social, paid, content all operating off the same shared brain. Voice and message consistency improves dramatically.

Beyond 90 days: Optimization. A/B testing with AI hypothesis generation, lifecycle marketing automation, ABM-at-scale.

Team-level fluency requirements

What every marketer should know

How to set up and use a shared Claude Project. Foundational.

How to write a marketing brief in Claude. See our guide.

How to edit AI content so it does not sound like AI. See our editorial guide.

How to use AI-generated outputs as drafts, not finished work.

Metrics that matter

How to prove the AI rollout worked

Content output per FTE. Should double in 90 days.

Time-from-brief-to-published. Should compress 50-60%.

Brand voice consistency. Measurable via internal audit.

Pipeline contribution. Lagging indicator but the one CEOs care about.

— Bill Colbert, Treetop Growth Strategy
